incident_reconstruction.reconstruction: The vehicle was involved in a frontal collision with another vehicle or object. The extensive damage to the front bumper, bonnet, headlights, and radiator grille indicates a high-energy impact. The damage pattern suggests that the Kia Seltos was likely stationary or moving slowly when hit by another vehicle, or it collided with a stationary object.
reconstruction_bullets: The severe damage to the front-end components, including the bonnet, front bumper, headlight_left, headlight_right, and radiator_grille, indicates a significant frontal impact.
The extent of the damage is consistent with a high-speed collision or a collision with a rigid object, given the deformation of the bonnet and the involvement of the radiator grille.

The claimant's story about hitting a vehicle in front that stopped suddenly could explain the frontal impact, but the lack of information about the other vehicle or obstacle raises questions about the completeness of the incident description.

storyboard_panels.title: Pre-Incident
storyboard_panels.description: The Kia Seltos was being driven on a road when the driver claims to have encountered an obstacle, leading to a sudden stop and subsequent collision with the vehicle in front.

storyboard_panels.title: Point of Impact
storyboard_panels.description: The Kia Seltos collided with the vehicle in front or an obstacle, resulting in severe damage to the front-end components.

storyboard_panels.title: Immediate Aftermath
storyboard_panels.description: The vehicle was found on the side of the road with significant front-end damage, indicating a potentially high-impact collision.

storyboard_panels.title: Evidence Summary
storyboard_panels.description: The physical evidence shows a severe frontal impact, consistent with a high-energy collision. However, details about the other vehicle or obstacle involved are lacking.

inconsistencies: The claimant's story lacks detail about the other vehicle or obstacle involved in the collision.
incident_reconstruction.summary: Most Probable: Front impact | Confidence: 70% | Story Match: Yes

decision_reasons: Fraud score of 39 is close to the escalate threshold and indicates potential workshop inflation (FS-004)

Claimant's story lacks detail about the other vehicle or obstacle involved in the collision, raising inconsistencies (inconsistencies: ["The claimant's story lacks detail..."])
Workshop 'Anna Motors' has appeared in 1 prior claim with an average estimate 101% higher than AI assessment, indicating a potential fraud scheme (FS-004)
reasoning_trail: Step 1: Damage Assessment indicates severe front-end damage consistent with a high-speed collision (overall_severity: Severe)
Step 2: Fraud Intelligence reveals a low fraud score but flags potential workshop inflation (fraud_score: 39, indicators: ['FI-INF-001', 'FS-004'])
Step 3: Incident Reconstruction confirms damage matches the story but notes inconsistencies in the claimant's account (damage_matches_story: True, inconsistencies: ["..."])
Step 4: Context & Policy verification confirms location and policy coverage (location_verified: True, policy_coverage_note: No obvious coverage exclusions...)
Step 5: Historical Precedents suggest similar cases have been escalated due to workshop inflation and inconsistencies (HIST-019, HIST-014)
Step 6: Settlement Calculation is not applicable due to escalation decision
Step 7: Final Decision is to escalate the claim due to potential workshop inflation and inconsistencies
